input_dict.current_prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.

output.query: I'm Still Here album artist genre

retrieved_doc_snippets: I'm Still Here (album) | I'm Still Here is the fifth and final studio album by American country music artist Mindy McCready. It was released on March 23, 2010, via Linus Entertainment. The album includes the single "I'm Still Here," as well as re-recordings of her 1996 singles "Guys Do It All the Time" and "Ten Thousand Angels." Mindy performed on "Fox & Friends" to promote the album in April 2010. This is McCready's final release before her death in 2013.

You're Still Here | "You're Still Here" is a song recorded by American country music artist Faith Hill. It was released in June 2003 as the fourth single from her fifth album "Cry" (2002). The song reached #28 on the "Billboard" Hot Country Singles & Tracks chart. The song was written by Matraca Berg and Aimee Mayo.

Still Standing (Monica album) | Still Standing is the sixth studio album by American R&B recording artist Monica. It was released on March 19, 2010 by J Records. Recording for the album began in 2007, in which the singer intended to take a more traditional approach to the genre and take away any "gimmicks." As executive producer of the album Monica enlisted a variety of producers including involvement by Missy Elliott, Bryan-Michael Cox, Stargate, and Jermaine Dupri. The song was released December 13, 2005, two months prior to the album release, and garnered frequent radio play in anticipation of the upcoming album. Evans Blue's official MySpace explains the meaning of this song as well as others by the group. A music video was also produced for "Cold (But I'm Still Here)." Though still mostly rooted in the death metal genre, this album takes the melodic death metal direction of 1993's "North from Here" a step further with elements of traditional heavy metal and doom metal, along with elements of the goth rock-inspired style that the band would take on with subsequent releases, following bassist/vocalist Taneli Jarva's  ...[truncated]

Idol Tryouts | Idol Tryouts is the second various artists compilation album released by Michigan-based label Ghostly International. Unlike the label's previous sampler "Disco Nouveau", "Idol Tryouts" contains music solely from artists signed to Ghostly International. Musical genres represented here span techno, electro, electroclash and tech house.

retrieved_doc_snippets: Ten Thousand Angels (song) | "Ten Thousand Angels" is a debut song written by Billy Henderson and Steven Dale Jones, and recorded by American country music artist Mindy McCready. It was released on April 1, 1996 as the first single and title track from the album "Ten Thousand Angels". The song reached #6 on the "Billboard" Hot Country Singles & Tracks chart.

I'm Still Here (album) | I'm Still Here is the fifth and final studio album by American country music artist Mindy McCready. It was released on March 23, 2010, via Linus Entertainment. The album includes the single "I'm Still Here," as well as re-recordings of her 1996 singles "Guys Do It All the Time" and "Ten Thousand Angels." Mindy performed on "Fox & Friends" to promote the album in April 2010. This is McCready's final release before her death in 2013.

Mindy McCready | Malinda Gayle "Mindy" McCready (November 30, 1975 – February 17, 2013) was an American country music singer. Active from 1995 until her death in 2013, she recorded a total of five studio albums. Her debut album, 1996's "Ten Thousand Angels", was released on BNA Records and was certified 2× Platinum by the RIAA, while 1997's "If I Don't Stay the Night" was certified Gold. 1999's "I'm Not So Tough", her final album for BNA, was less successful, and she left the label. The song was released on July 15, 1996, and reached the No. 1 spot on the "Billboard" Hot Country Singles & Tracks chart on September 14 of that year. It is McCready's first and only Number One hit. The song was the second single from McCready's double-platinum selling debut album "Ten Thousand Angels". A video was also issued of t ...[truncated]

Maybe He'll Notice Her Now | "Maybe He'll Notice Her Now" is a song written by Tim Johnson and recorded by American country music artist Mindy McCready featuring Richie McDonald. It was released on January 6, 1997 as the third single from McCready's double-platinum selling album "Ten Thousand Angels". The song reached number 18 on the "Billboard" Hot Country Singles & Tracks chart.

Ten Thousand Angels | Ten Thousand Angels is the 1996 debut album of American country music singer Mindy McCready. It was released on BNA Records and sold two million copies being certified double platinum by the RIAA. The album peaked at #5 on the US country charts, and producing four chart singles on the country charts. The first single, which was the title track, reached #6, and was followed by her only #1 hit, "Guys Do It All the Time". Following this song was "Maybe He'll Notice Her Now", w ...[truncated]

A Girl's Gotta Do (What a Girl's Gotta Do) | "A Girl's Gotta Do (What a Girl's Gotta Do)" is a song written by Robert Byrne and Rick Bowles, and recorded by American country music artist Mindy McCready. It was released in February 1997 as the fourth single from the album "Ten Thousand Angels". The song reached number 4 on the "Billboard" Hot Country Singles & Tracks chart.
